checks.finding: The quoted-heredoc plus named-environment boundary is justified against REQ-001 and preserves the canonical HMAC behavior required by REQ-002.
The requirements and design record no open questions, and layer specifications assign maintainers as owner.
Python 3 and the PowerShell .NET verifier remain existing runtimes with no new dependency.
The design, security, and acceptance-test documents preserve token format and fail-closed consent while mapping REQ-001 through REQ-005 to TEST-001 through TEST-007.
The verifier operates on a bounded token and SHA-256 digest; no performance-sensitive service or scale surface is introduced.
Local and existing CI execution, no-network/no-credential constraints, rollback signal, and reviewed revert are specified.
There is no persisted data or migration; token format, message order, exit codes, consent labels, and bundle contract remain unchanged.
The Bash parser, environment, Python verifier, consent decision, bundle writer, focused fixtures, and PowerShell parity path are identified.
The correction moves HMAC operands from generated source to named environment data without expanding scope.
TEST-001 through TEST-007 provide concrete target suites and outcomes for all acceptance paths.
Only a token satisfying nonce, TTL, repository binding, and constant-time HMAC verification may select sudo consent; all other paths deny before bundle creation.
